Critically, PyTorch requires index.shape == output.shape, and output.shape == index.shape; MindSpore output shape is input_params.shape[:axis] + input_indices.shape[batch_dims:] + input_params.shape[axis+1:], and with batch_dims=0, that equals [10] + [5,5] + [5] = [10,5,5] — same as index.shape.
